engaged_works.summary: This on-disk text is being wired into the Source tab as a citation witness for Yitzhak Y. Melamed, Yitzhak Y. Melamed A Companion To Spinoza 2021. The entry is intentionally minimal: it records the routed file and basic bibliographic shape so the site can surface the source while fuller review waits. A later pass should tighten the title, edition, and passage coverage against the on-disk witness.

core_thesis: Melamed reads Spinoza as an uncompromising metaphysical rationalist: substance is absolutely infinite and self-caused, while modes follow from it with strict necessity, so contingency and teleology are projections of inadequate understanding ([Spinoza's Metaphysics, ch. 1](cite://melamed/spinozas-metaphysics/1); [Spinoza's Metaphysics, ch. 2](cite://melamed/spinozas-metaphysics/2)). He further insists that the attributes are genuine expressions of substance rather than mere aspects relative to finite minds. On this reading, Spinoza's system is neither dualist nor voluntarist, but a doctrine of immanent dependence.
